%I A______ (placeholder)

%S 1,4,9,17,32,60,113,213,401,755,1421,2675,5036,9482,17849,33598,63247,
%T 119050

%N BFS layer sizes of the 3D right-corner orthoscheme reflection group with two equal and one distinct perpendicular legs (legs (1,1,2)).

%C The 3D right-corner orthoscheme has three mutually perpendicular legs at a vertex V_0. W = <R_0, R_1, R_2, R_3> is generated by reflections across the four faces; a(n) is the number of distinct affine isometries at minimal W-word-length n.

%C Values computed by exact-rational BFS on legs (a, b, c) = (1, 1, 2) through depth 17. Independent BFS on (1, 1, 3) and (2, 2, 3) through depth 14 yields the same initial terms, supporting (but not proving) dependence only on the equal/distinct symmetry pattern of the legs.

%C Empirically a(n+1)/a(n) approaches a limit between 1.88 and 1.92, strictly between the polynomial-growth case (legs all equal) and the rate 2 of the pairwise-distinct case.

%C Direct linear-algebra tests on depth-18 data rule out a linear recurrence over Q of order <= 9. No closed form is currently known.

%H V. Bonfioli, <a href="https://github.com/elvec1o/pythagorean-reflection-sequence">The Universal Right-Triangle Reflection Sequence (Unequal Legs)</a>, 2026.

%Y Cf. A______ (3D cube corner, legs (1,1,1)), A______ (3D pairwise distinct legs).
%Y 2D analogue: A______ (universal right-triangle reflection sequence).

%K nonn,hard,more

%O 0,2

%E More terms needed.

%A Vico Bonfioli, 2026
